---
title: "Machine Learning"
description: "A public-facing hub showing where the site's math modules reappear in machine learning models, training pipelines, and theory questions."
page-type: application-index
status: published
update-status: current
level: bridge
topic: machine-learning
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd
  - /topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd
  - /topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Map core math modules to recurring ML objects and tasks."
  - "Choose a first ML-facing reading path based on current interest."
  - "See where the site already has real ML bridge content."
applications:
  - machine-learning
  - machine-learning-theory
keywords:
  - machine learning
  - applications
  - roadmap
  - theory bridge
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: semiannual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
---

## Why This Section Exists

Many readers do not want math in the abstract. They want to know:

- where it shows up in ML
- which math topics matter first
- what to read next if they care about theory rather than only implementation

This hub answers those questions without trying to become a full ML textbook.

The rule for this section is simple:

every ML page should point back to the exact math objects it uses.

## What Machine Learning Keeps Reusing

Across models, training pipelines, and theory papers, the same mathematical objects keep returning:

- vectors, linear maps, projections, and low-rank structure
- probability, conditioning, and concentration
- estimators, validation, and generalization language
- optimization objectives, gradients, and regularization
- graph and spectral structure in structured or relational data

If you can identify those objects quickly, ML papers stop feeling like disconnected tricks.

::: {.meta-strip}
::: {.meta-box}
**Best Starting Math**
[Linear Algebra](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd), [Probability](../../topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd), [Statistics](../../topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd)
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Best Entry For Theory**
[AI / ML Theory Roadmap](../../roadmaps/ai-ml-theory.qmd)
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Best Entry For Papers**
[Paper Lab](../../paper-lab/index.qmd)
:::
:::

## Start Here By Interest

### If You Want The Shortest Math-to-ML Entry

Start in this order:

1. [Linear Algebra](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd)
2. [Probability](../../topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd)
3. [Statistics](../../topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd)
4. [AI / ML Theory Roadmap](../../roadmaps/ai-ml-theory.qmd)

### If You Want The Canonical Bridge Route Inside This Section

Start in this order:

1. [Supervised Learning, Losses, and Empirical Risk](supervised-learning-losses-and-empirical-risk.qmd)
2. [Optimization for Machine Learning](optimization-for-machine-learning.qmd)
3. [Generalization, Overfitting, and Validation](generalization-overfitting-and-validation.qmd)
4. [Regularization, Implicit Bias, and Model Complexity](regularization-implicit-bias-and-model-complexity.qmd)
5. [Uncertainty Calibration and Predictive Confidence](uncertainty-calibration-and-predictive-confidence.qmd)

This is the cleanest first bridge from the site's math modules into recurring ML questions.

### If You Want Concrete Model Components First

Start with the linear-algebra-heavy bridges:

1. [Vector Mixtures in Embeddings and Attention](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/vector-mixtures-in-embeddings-and-attention.qmd)
2. [Learned Linear Projections in Transformers](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/learned-linear-projections-in-transformers.qmd)
3. [PCA Through SVD](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/pca-through-svd.qmd)

### If You Want Theory-Flavored ML

Start with:

1. [Linear Regression Through Projection](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/linear-regression-through-projection.qmd)
2. [Probability](../../topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd)
3. [Statistics](../../topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd)
4. [AI / ML Theory Roadmap](../../roadmaps/ai-ml-theory.qmd)

## Application Families

### Linear Models, Embeddings, and Projections

These pages show the most reusable linear-algebra layer inside modern ML:

- [Representation Learning and Geometry of Embeddings](representation-learning-and-geometry-of-embeddings.qmd)
- [Linear Probes and Representation Diagnostics](linear-probes-and-representation-diagnostics.qmd)
- [Vector Mixtures in Embeddings and Attention](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/vector-mixtures-in-embeddings-and-attention.qmd)
- [Learned Linear Projections in Transformers](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/learned-linear-projections-in-transformers.qmd)
- [Linear Regression Through Projection](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/linear-regression-through-projection.qmd)
- [PCA Through SVD](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/pca-through-svd.qmd)
- [Low-Dimensional Subspace Models](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/low-dimensional-subspace-models.qmd)

Use this family when you want to see how vectors, matrices, orthogonality, and low-rank structure become real model components.

### Transformers, Prompting, and In-Context Learning

Use this family when you want to understand how prompts act like temporary task descriptions and why attention-based models can sometimes behave like small learners at inference time:

- [Backpropagation and Computation Graphs](backpropagation-and-computation-graphs.qmd)
- [Attention, Softmax, and Weighted Mixtures](attention-softmax-and-weighted-mixtures.qmd)
- [In-Context Learning and Linearization](in-context-learning-and-linearization.qmd)

### Graph and Spectral ML

Use this family when the model depends on graph structure, diffusion, or repeated linear updates:

- [Graph Diffusion and Message Passing](graph-diffusion-and-message-passing.qmd)
- [Oversmoothing, Depth, and Graph Sampling](oversmoothing-depth-and-graph-sampling.qmd)
- [Graph Rewiring, Homophily, and Heterophily](graph-rewiring-homophily-and-heterophily.qmd)
- [Long-Range Dependence and Oversquashing in Graphs](long-range-dependence-and-oversquashing-in-graphs.qmd)
- [Spectral Modes in Consensus and Graphs](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/spectral-modes-in-consensus-and-graphs.qmd)
- [Paper Lab: Spectral Clustering and Graph Modes](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/paper-lab/spectral-clustering-and-graph-modes.qmd)

### Training, Complexity, and Generalization

Use this family when the main question is not only fitting the sample, but understanding why training prefers some solutions over others:

- [Optimization for Machine Learning](optimization-for-machine-learning.qmd)
- [Generalization, Overfitting, and Validation](generalization-overfitting-and-validation.qmd)
- [Uncertainty Calibration and Predictive Confidence](uncertainty-calibration-and-predictive-confidence.qmd)
- [Regularization, Implicit Bias, and Model Complexity](regularization-implicit-bias-and-model-complexity.qmd)

### Similarity and Kernel ML

Use this family when the model is built from comparisons, similarities, or geometry induced by a kernel:

- [Kernel Methods and Similarity Geometry](kernel-methods-and-similarity-geometry.qmd)
- [Kernel Ridge and Gaussian-Process Intuition](kernel-ridge-and-gaussian-process-intuition.qmd)

### Surrogate Modeling and Sequential Design

Use this family when each evaluation is expensive and the model must decide where to gather information next:

- [Bayesian Optimization and Surrogate Modeling](bayesian-optimization-and-surrogate-modeling.qmd)
- [Kernel Ridge and Gaussian-Process Intuition](kernel-ridge-and-gaussian-process-intuition.qmd)
- [Experimental Design and Model Evaluation](../../topics/foundation/statistics/concepts/experimental-design-and-model-evaluation.qmd)

### Generative Modeling and Denoising

Use this family when the model learns to generate, denoise, or reverse a stochastic corruption process:

- [Diffusion Models and Denoising](diffusion-models-and-denoising.qmd)
- [Score Matching and the SDE View of Diffusion](score-matching-and-the-sde-view-of-diffusion.qmd)
- [Flow Matching and Transport Views of Generation](flow-matching-and-transport-views-of-generation.qmd)

### Research Bridges Already On The Site

These pages are useful when you want the first step from stable math into current ML-flavored research:

- [Paper Lab: Attention as Weighted Vector Mixture](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/paper-lab/attention-as-weighted-vector-mixture.qmd)
- [Paper Lab: Linear Operators in Modern AI Systems](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/paper-lab/linear-operators-in-modern-ai-systems.qmd)
- [Research Direction: Linear Operators in Modern ML](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/research/linear-operators-in-modern-ml.qmd)
- [Research Direction: Low-Dimensional Structure and Subspace Methods](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/research/low-dimensional-structure-and-subspace-methods.qmd)
- [Research Direction: Least Squares, Sketching, and Overparameterized Regression](../../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/research/least-squares-sketching-and-overparameterized-regression.qmd)

## How To Move Beyond The First Bridge Route

After the canonical bridge route, branch by family instead of reading the whole section linearly.

- choose `Transformers, Prompting, and In-Context Learning` if you care about sequence models and inference-time adaptation
- choose `Training, Complexity, and Generalization` if you care about why models fit and generalize
- choose `Graph and Spectral ML` if you care about structured data and message passing
- choose `Similarity and Kernel ML` if you care about nonlinear prediction through geometry
- choose `Generative Modeling and Denoising` if you care about diffusion, score, and transport views

The family blocks above are the main navigation layer. They are better first guides than a long page inventory.

## How To Use This Section

- Use `Topics` when you want the math itself.
- Use `Applications > Machine Learning` when you want the math-to-ML translation layer.
- Use [AI / ML Theory Roadmap](../../roadmaps/ai-ml-theory.qmd) when you want a dependency-aware study order.
- Use [Paper Lab](../../paper-lab/index.qmd) when you want to practice reading actual papers in parallel.

## Sources and Further Reading

- [CS229: Machine Learning](https://cs229.stanford.edu/index.html) - `First pass` - official current Stanford ML course hub showing the breadth of mainstream ML topics. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [CS 189 Syllabus](https://eecs189.org/sp26/syllabus/) - `First pass` - official Berkeley syllabus with a strong math-to-ML framing and prerequisite expectations. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Mathematics for Machine Learning](https://mml-book.github.io/) - `Second pass` - a stable math bridge for readers moving from foundations into ML-facing formulations. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [CS229T / Statistical Learning Theory](https://web.stanford.edu/class/cs229t/) - `Paper bridge` - a compact theory-facing anchor for readers aiming beyond introductory ML. Checked `2026-04-24`.
